exemplar.title: How do war poets across different historical periods use imagery, symbolism, and tone to shape evolving perceptions of war, from glorification to disillusionment?

detailedExemplarData.title: How do war poets across different historical periods use imagery, symbolism, and tone to shape evolving perceptions of war, from glorification to disillusionment?

subject.title: English Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Literature focuses exclusively on the study of literature, developing students' appreciation and critical analysis of literary works from various cultures, periods, and genres. Students examine poetry, prose, and drama through close reading, comparative analysis, and exploration of literary techniques and themes. The course cultivates sophisticated interpretive skills and deep understanding of how literature reflects and shapes human experience across different cultural contexts.

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with a clear purpose sustained throughout.
Research question is clearly stated and generally well connected to the discussion.
Use of sub-headings and introduction/conclusion maintains focus on the investigationâs aims.
weaknesses: Methodology lacks explicit rationale for source and poem selection.
Occasional digressions (e.g., references to drone warfare) dilute the tightness of focus.
Stylistic awkwardness in a few sentences reduces precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall, the investigation communicates its topic and purpose very clearly and sustains focus across sections, meriting the upper half of the 5â6 band. The research question is explicit and mostly well integrated, though its broad chronological span and minor digressions prevent the absolute top band. Methodological explanations are generally appropriate but would benefit from an explicit rationale. On balance, a score of 5 reflects strong performance with modest areas for improvement.

strengths: Demonstrates coherent understanding of each poemâs historical and literary context.
Integrates secondary critics (e.g., Roberts on propaganda, Cullhed on the Iliad) to support arguments.
Applies literary terminology (hyperbole, irony, elegiac tone) accurately in most instances.
weaknesses: Some contextual claims remain simplistic or under-developed (e.g., Bennettâs âmodernismâ).
Key theoretical terms are not consistently defined or contextualized.
Citation details (edition, line numbers) are occasionally missing, reducing scholarly rigor.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student exhibits very good knowledge and applies terminology effectively, placing the work comfortably in the upper zone of the 3â4 band and rubbing against 5â6. While minor lapses in precision and definition prevent full mastery, the integration of secondary sources and accurate vocabulary use justify a score of 5.

strengths: Research draws on a broad and relevant range of primary and secondary materials consistently tied to the research question.
Analysis offers clear comparative insights (e.g., Brooke vs. Owen, symbolism of gas imagery) that underpin the central thesis.
Discussion maintains a coherent argument about the evolution from glorification to disillusionment, with critical engagement in key sections.
weaknesses: Depth of analysis is unevenâLi Poâs poem and some broader cultural claims receive less sustained examination.
Some evaluations rely on summary of secondary sources rather than wholly original critique.
Conclusion restates findings without fully interrogating their wider implications.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation demonstrates strong critical thinking: research is largely relevant, analysis is substantive, and argumentation coherent and focused. Although a few sections are thinner and some evaluation could be more original, the overall quality warrants a high mark. A score of 10 reflects excellent performance with minor inconsistencies rather than fundamental weaknesses.

strengths: Report structure meets all formal requirements: title page, clear section headings, table of contents, works cited, and appendices.
Pages are numbered, word count and title are correctly placed, aiding navigation and compliance.
Signposting and subsection labels generally enhance readability.
weaknesses: Formatting is inconsistent: in-text citations vary in style and URLs break mid-word.
Abstract is missing, despite being implied by the structure.
Minor typographical issues (horizontal rules, misplaced symbols) interrupt professional presentation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While there are formatting and consistency issues, the overall presentation fully supports comprehension and conforms to IB requirements. The logical structure, presence of page numbers, word count and title justify awarding the maximum for Presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The methodâcomparative close reading of four primary poems supported by secondary criticismâis appropriate and mostly explained in the introduction.  A reasonable range of academic sources is cited, but selection is uneven: some references (e.g., Hart & Langstroth on napalm physics) have limited literary relevance, and citation formatting is inconsistent.  Appendices supply full poem texts, yet no rationale is given for editions chosen or for exclusion of other poems.  Overall, methodology is âmostly completeâ but shows patchiness in source relevance and scholarly apparatus, placing it at the lower end of the 3-4 band (score 3).

Pagination, word count and title are present, and MLA-style citations are attempted.  Nevertheless, formatting is inconsistent: in-text citations vary, long horizontal rule markers intrude on layout, several URLs break mid-word, and some mathematical symbols appear in place of punctuation (e.g., â$\overline{7}$â).  These issues reflect partial but imperfect application of layout conventions, placing the work in the 1-2 band with a score of 2.

The investigation draws on a fair spread of primary and secondary material, including journal articles and historical studies, most of which relate to the research question.  Source use is largely relevantâcriticism on WWI poetry and moral injury supports claims about Owen and Bennettâthough a few citations add limited value.  Because the majority of research choices enhance the exploration but completeness is not outstanding, the strand aligns with the lower end of the 7-9 band (score 7).

The title, sub-headings and opening paragraph communicate a clear intention to trace âwar poetryâs journeyâ through four specific poems.  The purposeâexamining imagery, symbolism and tone in order to chart a movement from glorification to disillusionmentâis explicitly stated and revisited in the conclusion.  Minor stylistic awkwardness (âwhereby writers record, analyseâ) occasionally blurs precision, yet the overall focus remains obvious to the reader.  Because the topic is presented accurately, with a coherent sense of purpose sustained across sections, the strand sits most comfortably in the 5-6 band.

A coherent argumentâthe shift from glorification to complexityâis maintained, and the student evaluates differing stances (Brookeâs patriotism vs. Owenâs denunciation; Bennettâs ironic ambivalence).  Some critical reflection on poetsâ historical circumstances strengthens the discussion, yet evaluation sometimes lapses into assertion (e.g., labeling Bennett âmodernistâ without critique) and the conclusion restates rather than interrogates findings.  These features indicate âgood argument development with partially critical evaluation,â aligning with a solid 6 in the 4-6 band.

Analysis moves beyond summary to interpret imagery, tone and symbolism across poems, offering comparative insights (e.g., contrasting Brookeâs pastoral nationalism with Owenâs visceral realism).  Many assertions are supported by textual evidence and secondary viewpoints.  However, depth is uneven: Li Poâs poem receives less sustained close reading, and broader claims about âcollective memoryâ are asserted rather than demonstrated.  The balance of strong passages and thinner sections typifies the upper zone of the 4-6 band, justifying a 6.

The essay follows the required structure: title page, abstract-style introduction, clear section headings, conclusion, works cited, and appendices.  Logical sequencing aids navigation, though sub-section labelling is occasionally inconsistent and the abstract is absent.  Minor repetition between sections suggests room for tighter organization.  The framework therefore meets key expectations but without exemplary precision, meriting the lower half of the 3-4 band (score 3).

The student demonstrates solid knowledge of each poemâs historical context and integrates several secondary critics to frame arguments (Cullhed on the Iliad, Roberts on Victorian masculinity).  Primary quotations are accurate and usually well-chosen.  Nonetheless, some contextual claims are simplistic or only partly supported (e.g., Bennett as âmodernistâ without exploration of Vietnam context), and a few sources are tangential.  This indicates good, but not exemplary, masteryâfitting the 3-4 band and warranting a score of 4.

Literary terminology such as âhyperbole,â âelegiac tone,â âpastoral imagery,â and âironyâ is applied correctly in most cases, showing a firm grasp of subject-specific vocabulary.  Occasional mis-phrasing (âspeaker finally recognizes that conflict involves more than suffering and mortalityâ) or redundancy slightly weakens precision, and key theoretical terms (e.g., âModernismâ) are not always defined.  Consistency is generally achieved, positioning the work securely within the 3-4 band (score 4).

The research question is explicitly formulated and appears on the title page.  It is reasonably focusedâlimiting itself to four named poems and three literary devicesâyet it still covers a wide chronological span and several complex concepts, which sometimes leads to broad, generalized commentary.  The discussion usually returns to the wording of the question, but occasional digressions (âHomerâs Iliad,â drone warfare) show that the focus is not always tight.  These features indicate competent but not consistently sharp alignment, meriting the upper zone of the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: The title is engaging and accurately reflects the investigationâs focus, immediately orienting the reader to the study of war poetryâs evolution.
Word count is clearly stated and complies with the IB requirement, aiding transparency of the investigationâs scope.
The research question is clear and appropriately connected to the discussion, but its broad chronological span dilutes focus. Consider narrowing terminology or period range.
The table of contents is present but there is no abstract section listed. Including an abstract would strengthen the reportâs structural completeness.
Entries in the contents table vary in punctuation and formatting (e.g., missing periods, inconsistent capitalization). Standardize labels for clarity and professionalism.
The introduction lacks an explicit methodology section explaining source selection and analytical approach. Add a clear methodology statement to meet IB requirements.
Phrasing such as âwhereby writers record, analyseâ is stylistically awkward. Revise for concision and clarity to improve flow.
The sub-heading clearly signals the shift to earlier poetry and aids navigationâgood use of signposting.
Li Poâs quoted lines are uncited by edition or line number. Include precise textual references to strengthen scholarly rigor.
An unexplained â\overline{4}â appears at the page footerâlikely an OCR or formatting error. Remove or replace with proper pagination.
The comparative link between Li Po and Owen is insightful. To deepen analysis, trace how specific imagery conventions evolve across eras.
The term âmodernistâ is used to describe Bennett without definition or contextual grounding. Define key theoretical terms to avoid ambiguity.
In-text citations vary in style (e.g., mixing authorâdate with narrative mention). Adopt a consistent citation convention throughout.
Integration of Robertsâs analysis of wartime propaganda is well executed and strengthens contextual understanding.
The section header âThe Atrocities of Conflictâ effectively contrasts Brookeâs idealism with Owenâs realismâclear and impactful.
Your analysis of gas imagery in Owenâs poem is vivid; make explicit how this supports your thesis on the evolution from glorification to disillusionment.
The application of PTSD research to Owenâs depiction is thorough, but clarify where student interpretation ends and source argument begins.
The section occasionally summarises secondary sources rather than critically evaluating them. Aim for more original critique to strengthen argument depth.
No rationale is provided for selecting precisely these four poems from a wide historical span. Justify poem selection in methodology.
Tense shifts between present and past weaken formal consistency (e.g., âemploysâ vs âportraysâ). Standardize tense for academic tone.
The conclusion summarizes findings effectively but does not critically evaluate broader implications. Incorporate reflective evaluation to deepen closure.
Bennett analysis offers a nuanced perspective on mechanized warfareâs psychological dualityâstrong engagement with modern poetry conventions.
The works cited list includes formatting inconsistencies (e.g., missing DOIs, broken URLs). Apply one citation style rigorously.
Appendices effectively include full texts, but the rationale for their separate placement is unclear. Explain appendix use in methodology.
Page numbering is consistently applied at the bottom of each page, facilitating navigation through the document.
